results.output:
I'll review `SubprocessSpendGate` and how callers invoke model/CLI paths to find any remaining spend bypass.Next I'll trace adapter and dispatch call paths for any model invocation that skips the gate.VERDICT: Not airtight — the gate is opt-in; parallel in-process HTTP spend paths never touch it.

RATIONALE: `SubprocessSpendGate.dispatch()` only meters work that voluntarily routes through it; it does not monkey-patch `subprocess`, wrap the process, or interpose on sockets. Any caller that uses raw `subprocess`/`Popen`/`os.system` for a model CLI, or any in-process HTTP/SDK client, skips preflight, the cost ledger, and governor updates entirely. The highest-likelihood live path in this repo is already-wired provider HTTP outside the H1D router, not a theoretical second shellout in `dispatch.py`.

BYPASS: `scripts/prompt_brain/model_adapters.py` — e.g. `OpenAIAdapter.execute()` POSTs to `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` via `urllib.request` (and the Anthropic adapter does the same class of call); neither path ever calls `SubprocessSpendGate.dispatch()`.
